steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The notes show North Pacific konbu rated moderate umami and arame rated high umami. The student wants to emphasize a difference between the two. The correct choice must contrast their umami intensities directly.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Although North Pacific konbu and arame are types of brown seaweed, the latter's umami flavor is more intense.': directly contrasts the two seaweeds by showing arame has higher umami than konbu. ✓
  • Choice B — 'Some types of brown seaweed, like North Pacific konbu and arame, trigger umami flavor in human taste buds.': groups both seaweeds together; emphasizes similarity, not difference. ✗
  • Choice C — 'While North Pacific konbu and arame contain umami flavor, umami can also be found in tuna and garlic.': shifts focus to other umami sources; does not contrast the two seaweeds. ✗
  • Choice D — 'North Pacific konbu is a type of brown seaweed, but so is arame.': notes both are brown seaweed; does not address umami intensity at all. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
